description:
## Goal

Replace per-callsite spec minting with one canonical spec per fn,
keyed on the LUB (element-wise Descr::union) of every observed input.
The canonical is the type-theoretic soundness floor; everything else
in this arc layers on top.

## What lands

- New data model in src/ir_typer.rs:
  - `Canonical { fid: FnId, lub_key: Vec<Descr>, sid: SpecId,
     refinements: Vec<SpecId> }` (refinements vec starts empty;
     populated in fz-rfn.2)
  - Replace `HashMap<(FnId, Vec<Descr>), FnTypes>` with
    `HashMap<FnId, Canonical>` as the primary registry index.
    `SpecRegistry` still hands out SpecIds for annotations; the
    underlying storage is canonical-per-fn.

- `walk_spec_for_discovery` becomes a LUB-accumulator: at each
  push_key for callee F, compute K = args→Descrs; merge into
  F.lub_key via element-wise Descr::union; if changed, re-walk F.

- Existing SCC + `widen()` at WIDEN_AT=3 handles scalar/atom-set
  widening for fixpoint termination. No new widening logic.

- Codegen reads canonical-only. ABI per slot derived from
  `ArgRepr::from_descr(canonical.lub_key[i])`. Tagged-everywhere
  for most fns at this stage (refinements come in rfn.2).

## What gets removed

- `fn ensure_any_keys` and its caller — canonical is the floor
  by construction.
- `naturally_opaque: HashSet<FnId>` and every read of it (codegen
  picker, etc.) — every reachable FnId has a canonical that
  handles opaque dispatch by definition.
- `tagged_return_specs`, `tagged_slot0_cont_specs`,
  `tagged_return_fns` HashMaps — per-spec ABI now derives
  directly from the spec's key via `ArgRepr::from_descr`. ~64
  references across ir_typer.rs + ir_codegen.rs disappear.
- The MakeClosure-target blanket reachable seed in
  `reachable_specs` (lines 2938-2953) — closures wire to
  canonical (always reachable transitively), refinements
  unreachable via opaque paths.
- The current SpecRegistry's two-tier exact/subsumption-minimal
  resolve becomes single-tier: lookup by FnId returns the
  canonical SpecId (refinement resolution is annotation-driven
  in rfn.2+).
- The parallel any-key body story — canonical IS the any-key.

## Expected effect

CLIF function count drops dramatically:
- fib_tailrec: 14 → 4
- ast_eval_env: 59 → ~10
- list_primitives: similar collapse

Spec count collapses to ~= number of distinct fns in the program
(plus continuation fns).

Performance regresses slightly because narrow ABI is gone
(everything Tagged); this is recovered in fz-rfn.2/3 via
refinements.

## fz-t45 dissolves

Closure values' body_fn_id wires to canonical's FuncId. Indirect
dispatch sig = all-Tagged i64. Canonical ABI = Tagged by
construction. Wire matches semantics. The TailCallClosure ABI
mismatch is structurally impossible after this lands.

## Acceptance

- All 618 unit + matrix fixtures pass with regenerated goldens.
- Spec count in expected.specs drops to ~1 per fn + helper conts.
- CLIF count drops to ~1 per fn + helper conts.
- fz-t45 repro program (each + closure with non-empty captures)
  produces correct output: `11 / 12 / 13`. Close fz-t45.
- No clippy warnings, fmt clean.

## See also

- fz-aiz.7 — annotation-driven reachable_specs (already landed).
- fz-aiz.3a-c — annotation arc (stays intact; canonical+
  refinement model rides on top).
- src/ir_codegen.rs:782 — ArgRepr::from_descr (the ABI projection
  this ticket centralizes around).
title: fz-rfn.1: canonical-first — LUB accumulation per fn (replaces per-callsite spec minting)
